prompt:
Fix all 5 unresolved Gemini Code Assist review comments on PR #315 (modules/media-server/recyclarr.nix):

1. HIGH/Security (L37-38): API keys are leaked via curl command-line args visible in /proc/<pid>/cmdline. Fix by using curl's --config or -K option to read headers from stdin instead. For example:
   cat <<EOF | curl -sf -K - "$url/api/v3/system/status"
   header = "X-Api-Key: $api_key"
   EOF
   Apply this pattern everywhere curl is called with -H "X-Api-Key: ..." in the script.

2. HIGH (L122): The call ensure_vvc_custom_format "$RADARR_URL" "$RADARR_API_KEY" "Radarr" "v3" is redundant because recyclarr.yml already adds VVC/H.266 rejection for Radarr via TRaSH Guides (trash_id 390455c22a9cac81a738f6cbad705c3c). Remove this line, keeping only the Sonarr call.

3. MEDIUM/Security (L138-150): The Prowlarr connection JSON payloads for Sonarr and Radarr use shell string interpolation with escaped quotes, which is fragile and injection-prone. Refactor into a reusable shell function and use jq to construct the JSON payload safely (similar to how jq is used elsewhere in the script).

4. MEDIUM (L46): The warning message 'WARNING: $name did not become ready in time, skipping.' is misleading because set -e means the script will exit when wait_for_api returns 1. Change to 'ERROR: $name did not become ready in time. Exiting.'

5. MEDIUM (L63): Quote the ${recyclarrConfig} variable in the cp command: cp "${recyclarrConfig}" "$STATE_DIR/recyclarr.yml"

Make sure the NixOS config still evaluates (nix build --dry-run or nix flake check if available). Run any pre-commit hooks. Create a PR targeting the PR #315 branch.
